prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Use the current frame-business-research-problem Skill at skills/frame-business-research-problem/. Follow its readiness, fidelity, managerial-framing, scholarly-positioning, evidence, boundary, and mode instructions. Use only the serialized case material.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": false,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "maintenance jobs",
    "travel times",
    "technician capacities",
    "eligibility",
    "cost and penalty weights"
  ],
  "domain": "OR",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "A planning deadline makes early feasibility operationally valuable",
      "source_needed": true,
      "status": "unsupported_claim"
    },
    {
      "claim": "An optimality certificate changes an approval or escalation decision",
      "source_needed": true,
      "status": "unsupported_claim"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "Compare methods on the same feasible set and objective",
    "Connect speed, quality, or certification to an explicit operational action before claiming value"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "unsupported_causal_claim",
    "hidden_major_assumption",
    "unsupported_market_claim"
  ],
  "id": "or-14",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "technician capacity",
      "job eligibility",
      "route continuity"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "technician routes",
      "jobs selected for service"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "minimize travel and missed-service penalty",
    "mechanism": "The heuristic emphasizes rapid feasible construction, whereas the exact method searches for improved routes and a bound that may eventually certify optimality.",
    "model_type": "exact-versus-heuristic capacitated maintenance routing",
    "state_variables": [],
    "uncertainty": "algorithm runtime and incumbent quality across instances"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "Exactness is always more valuable to managers",
    "A faster heuristic necessarily improves field performance"
  ],
  "prompt": "A maintenance-routing team can use a heuristic that returns feasible routes quickly or an exact method that may improve the objective and certify optimality with more computation. The supplied model does not state when routes must be released or what action a certificate changes. Frame the comparison without declaring either method managerially superior until a deadline, cadence, scale, or escalation pathway is specified.",
  "tags": [
    "exactness",
    "heuristic",
    "operational-pathway",
    "maintenance-routing"
  ],
  "title": "Operational pathway for exact versus heuristic planning value"
}
```
